title: Combinatorics, Conditional Probability, and Simulation · Worked Case: Monte Carlo
summary: Analyze staffing reliability, screening results, and queue risks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int monte carlo increment in a simulated operational risk model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.
Define discrete random variables and calculate expectation and variance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Monte Carlo evidence
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $35 with probability 0.35 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.
